extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping hemoptysis from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. hemoptysis appears only in r4 (priority 100): IF (leg_swelling AND palpitations AND pallor AND hemoptysis) THEN acuity := self_care, which sets the LOWEST acuity and has a dominating priority.

Counterexample with leg_swelling=true, palpitations=true, pallor=true, all others false:

Baseline (hemoptysis=false): r1 (leg_swelling, prio 2 -> urgent), r2 (pallor, prio 2 -> urgent), r3 (palpitations, prio 3 -> emergent) fire; r4 does not (hemoptysis false). Highest-priority matching rule is r3 (prio 3) -> acuity = emergent.

After flipping hemoptysis=true: r1, r2, r3 still fire, plus r4 now fires (all four conjuncts true) at priority 100 -> self_care. r4's priority 100 beats r3's priority 3, so acuity = self_care.

emergent -> self_care is a decrease in the ordered enum [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent]. Setting hemoptysis true lowered acuity, so monotonicity is VIOLATED. The witness assignment (the hemoptysis=true state yielding self_care) demonstrates the drop relative to its hemoptysis=false baseline (emergent).
